blocks.content: Infinite (3)

blocks.speaker: Emi
blocks.content: How foolish… truly foolish…!
He was the one who reached out first, who handed her coffee, only for her to coldly reject him.
As if turning away from the regret-filled scenes, Emi tightly shut her eyes.
Then, with all her might, she screamed.

blocks.speaker: Emi
blocks.content: Shirone!
Shirone smiled.
He was relieved that Emi had regained her memories.
At the very least, he could depart as Shirone, remaining in someone's memory until the very end.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Thank you, Emi.
Shirone's tears fell onto Emi's cheek.

blocks.speaker: Emi
blocks.content: Why are you crying, Shirone?

blocks.speaker: Emi
blocks.content: Something was wrong.
Then, the moment she saw the light of life gradually fading from Shirone's eyes, she realized.

blocks.speaker: Emi
blocks.content: Shirone! No!

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: I'm sorry. I couldn't keep my promise.
Shirone, holding Emi in his arms, fully unleashed the Immortal Function.
As his consciousness expanded infinitely, his sense of self began to fade, dissolving like ink in water.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Goodbye, everyone….
Father, mother, the members of the Ozent family, friends from school.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Like Master….

blocks.speaker: Harvest
blocks.content: Yes, and like that boy.
At the center of the 400 scattered students, Shirone lay collapsed.
He had vowed not to harm anyone.
And he had upheld that belief until the very end.
Kanis approached Harvest and checked his condition.

blocks.speaker: Kanis
blocks.content: How are you holding up?

blocks.speaker: Harvest
blocks.content: What do you think? I'm dying. Without a host, I can't survive.

blocks.speaker: Arin
blocks.content: Make a master-servant contract with me. Kanis won't be able to endure after exhausting his mental energy.

blocks.speaker: Harvest
blocks.content: Sorry, but that's not happening.

blocks.speaker: Arin
blocks.content: What? Why not? You'll vanish like this!

blocks.speaker: Harvest
blocks.content: My master is Kanis.
Harvest's words pierced Kanis's heart.
Now, it was his turn to raise the banner of his own beliefs.
Could he endure? If he faltered, both of them would lose their lives.

blocks.speaker: Kanis
blocks.content: Harvest. I'll take you as my subordinate. But I won't forgive betrayal a second time.

blocks.speaker: Harvest
blocks.content: Kukuku. Understood, Master.

blocks.speaker: Arin
blocks.content: Kanis! It's too dangerous! If something goes wrong…!
Arin stopped herself.
Kanis's liquid form was hardening as mud seeped into it.
Would he one day gain an unshakable heart? Like the image of Shirone she had seen through her mirror.
Kanis placed his hand on Harvest.
As the subordinate contract took effect, Harvest began siphoning what little life force remained.
Kanis's consciousness faded into darkness as his eyes slowly closed.
Alpheus stood somberly apart from Kanis and his group.
Though the students had narrowly escaped death, the losses were too great.

blocks.speaker: Alpheus
blocks.content: Headmaster.

blocks.speaker: Etella
blocks.content: Etella, I am a sinner.

blocks.speaker: Etella
blocks.content: Don't blame yourself too much. If a life where even mistakes are unforgivable is a sin, then the very existence of humanity is sinful.
Alpheus shook his head.
Everyone makes mistakes. But responsibility always follows.
Like Akein, he had no intention of running away.

blocks.speaker: Alpheus
blocks.content: I was the one who elevated dark magic to the ranks of grand magic. Harvest could be called revolutionary in the field of memory transfer. Abyss Nova will be registered as a regulated spell by the Magic Association.
Before Akein's corpse, Alpheus clasped his hands in mourning.

blocks.speaker: Alpheus
blocks.content: The eternal boy, Archmage Akein. The path of magic you walked will live on in future generations.
When Alpheus opened his eyes, he seemed lighter.

blocks.speaker: Etella
blocks.content: Headmaster, what do you plan to do now?

blocks.speaker: Alpheus
blocks.content: What can I say? I, too, am merely waiting for judgment. Rather than punishment, shouldn't we first honor today's hero?
Alpheus glanced around in search of Shirone.
The boy had a knack for astonishing people.
Even Alpheus couldn't confidently say he could teleport 400 people at once.

blocks.speaker: Alpheus
blocks.content: We have a problem!
Emi was sobbing near where Shirone had collapsed.
Sensing the unusual atmosphere, Alpheus and Etella quickened their steps.
In the distance, Iruki and Nade were running toward them.
But before they could arrive, Emi screamed first.

blocks.speaker: Emi
blocks.content: Shirone isn't breathing!
